prevalence.value: ~0.3-1% (workforce); ~10-15% of adult asthma attributable to work
prevalence.source: Occupational asthma epidemiology syntheses; WHO occupational risk context
prevalence.trend: stable; underdiagnosed due to limited occupational medicine services

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; high productivity loss and chronic asthma morbidity for affected workers
burden.mortalityRate: Low; severe uncontrolled asthma risk exists

vulnerablePopulations: informal workers without occupational health coverage
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Work-related symptom pattern plus objective confirmation (spirometry/peak flow variation across work-rest cycles); specialist evaluation for sensitizer testing when possible

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands (subset of adult asthma)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best with exposure control and guideline-based asthma therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinically suspected; confirmation limited outside tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exposure reduction/removal, workplace controls, PPE; ICS-based asthma therapy; documentation for occupational compensation when applicable
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Informal workforce barriers, underdiagnosis, limited occupational medicine services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High-impact prevention when exposures are controlled; standard asthma medications are widely available
